title: Avoid hardcoding integration endpoints in the top-level Terraform module outputs
summary: The top-level product Terraform module's terraform/outputs.tf hardcodes every coordinator integration-endpoint name as a string literal in its `provides` and `requires` outputs, instead of referencing the coordinator sub-module's provides/requires outputs (coordinator/terraform/outputs.tf). Unlike the sibling tempo-operators module -- which already wires several of its outputs to module.<coordinator>.provides/.requires -- the pyroscope top-level module uses NO module references at all. This duplication is a drift hazard: change an endpoint name in the coordinator module and the top-level literal silently goes stale. This is the same class of issue as tempo-operators#324 ('Avoid hardcoding endpoints in the TF module').
why_good_first_issue: Terraform-only, single-file, mechanical change with an unambiguous done state and no charm/Python/architecture knowledge required. The correct target values already exist in coordinator/terraform/outputs.tf, and the exact desired pattern is demonstrated in the sibling repo (tempo-operators/terraform/outputs.tf), so the contributor mostly replaces literals with module references. Maintainers already labelled the identical tempo issue 'good first issue'.
affected_files: terraform/outputs.tf (top-level product module); reference: coordinator/terraform/outputs.tf, coordinator/charmcraft.yaml
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/pyroscope-operators ~/work/pyroscope-operators'; cat -n terraform/outputs.tf  (all provides/requires values are bare string literals); cat -n coordinator/terraform/outputs.tf  (submodule already exposes provides{} and requires{}); grep -n 'module "pyroscope_coordinator"' -A1 terraform/main.tf  (source=../coordinator/terraform).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection. terraform/outputs.tf hardcodes everything: `provides` (lines 21-24) = pyroscope_cluster="pyroscope-cluster", grafana_dashboard="grafana-dashboard", grafana_source="grafana-source", metrics_endpoint="metrics-endpoint"; `requires` (lines 30-38) = logging, ingress, certificates, send-remote-write, receive-datasource, catalogue, workload-tracing, charm-tracing, s3 -- all literals, zero module references. coordinator/terraform/outputs.tf already exposes matching maps: provides{} (lines 5-12) = grafana_dashboard, send_datasource, metrics_endpoint, pyroscope_cluster; requires{} (lines 14-23) = certificates, ingress, logging, charm_tracing, workload_tracing, s3, catalogue. A real drift ALSO exists: the top-level advertises provides.grafana_source="grafana-source" but the coordinator submodule's provides{} omits grafana_source and instead has send_datasource="send-datasource"; coordinator/charmcraft.yaml defines BOTH grafana-source (line 74) and send-datasource (line 85) as real endpoints, so the submodule output is itself incomplete. For comparison, tempo-operators/terraform/outputs.tf already uses module references (e.g. module.tempo_coordinator.provides.provide_cmr_mesh), proving this is the intended pattern.
fix_hint: In terraform/outputs.tf, replace the literals in `provides`/`requires` with references to the coordinator sub-module outputs, e.g. pyroscope_cluster = module.pyroscope_coordinator.provides.pyroscope_cluster, grafana_dashboard = module.pyroscope_coordinator.provides.grafana_dashboard, logging = module.pyroscope_coordinator.requires.logging, etc. As part of the fix, reconcile the grafana_source/send_datasource gap: add grafana_source = "grafana-source" to coordinator/terraform/outputs.tf provides{} (both endpoints exist in charmcraft.yaml) so the top-level output can reference it. Watch the hyphen-vs-underscore key spellings (top-level send-remote-write/receive-datasource vs submodule send_remote_write/receive_datasource). Run `terraform fmt` and `terraform validate` in terraform/ if a TF toolchain is present.
notes: Inspection-tier (Terraform, no unit env to run). Self-identified because the only label-eligible open issue, #285 ('use nginx module from charmlibs', tagged good first issue), is now OBSOLETE: coordinator/src/charm.py:16 already imports `from charmlibs.nginx_k8s import NginxConfig, TLSConfigManager` and no old nginx lib remains under coordinator/lib, so that issue is effectively done and was deliberately not picked. Directly mirrors tempo-operators#324, which maintainers accepted as a good-first-issue.

title: Add missing `description` fields to top-level Terraform module outputs
summary: None of the outputs in the top-level terraform/outputs.tf (app_names, provides, requires) carry a `description` argument, so the module's published interface is undocumented in `terraform output`, generated docs, and the registry. The sibling tempo-operators module documents every one of these same outputs with a `description` (e.g. 'All application names which make up this product module', 'All Juju integration endpoints where the charm is the provider/requirer'). Adding the equivalent descriptions makes the pyroscope module consistent and self-documenting.
why_good_first_issue: Tiny, self-contained, documentation-only edit to a single Terraform file with a ready-made reference (tempo's outputs.tf) to copy wording from. No code, tests, or architecture understanding needed; done state is unambiguous (each output has a description).
affected_files: terraform/outputs.tf (top-level product module); reference: tempo-operators terraform/outputs.tf
repro_steps: multipass exec cos -- bash -lc 'git clone --depth 80 https://github.com/canonical/pyroscope-operators ~/work/pyroscope-operators'; grep -c 'description' terraform/outputs.tf  (returns 0); cat -n terraform/outputs.tf  (confirm app_names/provides/requires have no description); compare with tempo-operators/terraform/outputs.tf where each output has a description line.
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection. `grep -c description terraform/outputs.tf` returns 0 -- the three outputs app_names (lines 1-17), provides (19-26) and requires (28-39) have no `description` argument. By contrast tempo-operators/terraform/outputs.tf gives each output a description ('All application names which make up this product module' on app_names; 'All Juju integration endpoints where the charm is the provider' / '... the requirer' on provides/requires), so the pyroscope module is the inconsistent/undocumented one.
fix_hint: Add a `description = "..."` line to each output block in terraform/outputs.tf, reusing tempo-operators' wording: app_names -> 'All application names which make up this product module'; provides -> 'All Juju integration endpoints where the charm is the provider'; requires -> 'All Juju integration endpoints where the charm is the requirer'. Run `terraform fmt` in terraform/ to keep alignment.
notes: Inspection-tier (Terraform). Independent of and smaller than the hardcoding cleanup (Pick 1), though both touch the same file -- could be combined into one PR if desired. Self-identified because the open-issue pool is thin/blocked: #285 is obsolete (nginx charmlib already adopted); #271 (remove cpu_limit/memory_limit) is explicitly future-work gated on 'track 3' and cross-repo coordination; #276 is a design-y TF storage enhancement; #315 is integration-tier and blocked on an upstream decision (flagged separately).
